mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, forcing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, with forward return accuracy dropping below 48% in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading falling below 1.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank accommodation, producing immediate positive equity returns within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of low-inflation trigger events, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent rate decisions.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening aggregate demand and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll declines correlate with positive 60-day returns in over 55% of observations, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust domestic consumption momentum, which directly lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 58% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply chain stress and margin erosion, forcing immediate negative equity returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances, or the regime-filtered signal accuracy falls below 50%.
